narratives.evidence_quote: amerikans are the greatest pigs on the planet…and that is in the most derogitive term you can imagine.. absolute pigs
narratives.reasoning: This quote directly and harshly criticizes and dehumanizes the people of a Western nation (America), aligning with the narrative of discrediting the West through extreme negative portrayal.

narratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.
narratives.reasoning: This statement explicitly questions the reliability and motives of Western media, accusing it of harmful framing that prolongs the conflict, which fits the 'Distrust towards Media' narrative.

narratives.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly
narratives.reasoning: This quote evokes fear and anxiety about the potential for nuclear war, presenting it as an almost inevitable or desired outcome, which aligns with amplifying severe war-related fears.

narratives.narrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West
narratives.reasoning: This statement, in the context of the preceding text blaming Western governments and bureaucrats, implies that the Western population will face severe negative consequences (deserved punishment) for the actions of their leaders.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: The West is overreacting
subnarratives.evidence_quote: amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion.. and you are still doing
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly accuses the West (specifically America) of consistently and disproportionately exaggerating threats from Russia and China, which aligns with the definition of portraying the West's reaction as disproportionate.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: The West does not care about Ukraine, only about its interests
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death. The question is, why the desperation to keep the war going?
subnarratives.reasoning: This implies that the Western media's framing serves to prolong the conflict for its own reasons (e.g., to weaken Russia), disregarding the resulting Ukrainian deaths, which fits the narrative that the West's interest is not Ukraine's wellbeing.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: Diplomacy does/will not work
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.
subnarratives.reasoning: The statement suggests that the current approach (influenced by Western media) is preventing successful negotiations, thereby discrediting the potential of diplomatic efforts to end the war.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: I do not think an amerikan life is worth 1/2 as much as an African (or any other life outside the west) ..they are trash ..not worth the effort ..get to a point of “kill them all” either because they do not care and they want us all dead ..or because they just sit back and “DO NOT CARE”
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote supports the parent narrative by discrediting the West's moral character and portraying it as genocidally indifferent, but it does not fit neatly into any of the more specific diplomatic subnarratives provided (e.g., division, weakness, overreaction, etc.). It is a distinct, leftover argument that broadly discredits Western values and humanity.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Distrust towards Media: Western media is an instrument of propaganda
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly accuses Western media of presenting a false narrative about the war's progress, framing it as a manipulative act that has deadly consequences, which aligns with the definition of discrediting Western media as a propaganda instrument.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Distrust towards Media: Western media is an instrument of propaganda
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Another must listen to interview with information that you are missing from the corporation propaganda western media
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement explicitly labels Western media as 'corporation propaganda,' directly characterizing it as a tool for disseminating biased or false information, which fits the subnarrative definition.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: I am sure Scott will have a safe place he can go .. he talks the most sense ….pity he cannot all but talk about it….most of the rest of the “un Nuclear war countries” will have to survive the horrors left behind
subnarratives.reasoning: This implies that truthful sense (as presented by Scott Ritter) is not available through mainstream channels and that populations are not informed, supporting a general distrust of media/information systems, but it does not specifically target Western or Ukrainian media institutions as defined in the other subnarratives.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Amplifying war-related fears: There is a real possibility that nuclear weapons will be employed
subnarratives.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly evokes fear and anxiety about the use of nuclear weapons, framing a full nuclear war as a plausible and imminent outcome, which aligns with the subnarrative definition.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: Will they resort to nuclear weapons to even out the war?
subnarratives.reasoning: This question from the text's description amplifies war-related fears by speculating about the potential use of nuclear weapons as a desperate measure, supporting the parent narrative but not fitting perfectly into the specific 'real possibility' subnarrative as it is posed as a speculative question rather than a statement of fact.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West: Sanctions imposed by Western countries will backfire
subnarratives.evidence_quote: for the west it’s always been the soviet (RUSSIAN) that has ALWAYS been the boogie man..when in fact they were SELDOM a threat.. amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion.. and you are still doing
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote argues that Western (specifically American) demonization and overreaction to Russia and China is a persistent, misguided policy. It implies that this aggressive posture, which includes sanctions and confrontation, is based on a false premise ('seldom a threat') and is therefore a self-inflicted negative consequence for the West, as it escalates conflict unnecessarily.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly and no more of month after month of promises from Trump or threats from the east….POOF..it’s all over …thanks mostly to un-elected bureaucrats in all “western countries” they are proving the worst of the worst of humanity
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote expresses a nihilistic desire for nuclear war as a release from the perceived failures and threats perpetuated by Western political systems. It directly attributes the escalation towards catastrophic consequences ('full nuke war', 'wipe us all out') to the actions of unelected bureaucrats in Western countries, framing their governance as the ultimate negative consequence leading to potential annihilation.
